(d) mid point of CE is ______

question_text: A horizontal line and a vertical line always intersect at right angles.

provenance_excerpt: A horizontal line and a vertical line always intersect at right angles.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If the arms of an angle on the paper are increased, the angle increases.
answer_explanation: The measure of an angle depends on the rotation between its arms, not on their lengths. Increasing the length of arms does not change the angle.
provenance_excerpt: If the arms of an angle on the paper are increased, the angle increases.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If the arms of an angle on the paper are decreased, the angle decreases.
answer_explanation: Similar to above, decreasing the length of arms does not change the angle measure.
provenance_excerpt: If the arms of an angle on the paper are decreased, the angle decreases.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Two line segments may intersect at two points.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Many lines can pass through two given points.
answer_explanation: Through two distinct points, exactly one line can pass. This is a basic postulate of geometry.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Only one line can pass through a given point.
answer_explanation: Through a single point, infinitely many lines can pass.

An angle is formed when we have
(a) two rays with a common end-point
(b) two line segments with a common end-point

(d) Sectors OAC and OPB shaded
(e) Smaller segment formed by CP shaded
answer_explanation: Chords are line segments with both endpoints on the circle. Radii connect the center to points on the circle. A diameter is a chord passing through the center.

(b) Name all radii of the circle.

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Can we have two acute angles whose sum is
(a) an acute angle? Why or why not?
(b) a right angle? Why or why not?
(c) an obtuse angle? Why or why not?
(d) a straight angle? Why or why not?
(e) a reflex angle? Why or why not?
answer_key:
(a) Yes (e.g., 30° + 40° = 70°)
(b) Yes (e.g., 45° + 45° = 90°)
(c) Yes (e.g., 50° + 60° = 110°)
(d) No (max sum of two acute angles = 89° + 89° = 178° < 180°)
(e) No (sum of two acute angles is always less than 180°, which is less than any reflex angle)
answer_explanation: Acute angles are less than 90°. Their sum can be acute, right, or obtuse, but never reaches 180° or more.
provenance_excerpt:
Can we have two acute angles whose sum is
(a) an acute angle? Why or why not?
(b) a right angle? Why or why not?
(c) an

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Can we have two obtuse angles whose sum is
(a) a reflex angle? Why or why not?
(b) a complete angle? Why or why not?
answer_key:
(a) Yes (e.g., 100° + 100° = 200°, which is reflex)
(b) Yes (e.g., 180° + 180° = 360° but obtuse angles must be less than 180°, so actually No. Max = 179° + 179° = 358° < 360°)
answer_explanation: (a) Yes, since two obtuse angles can sum to more than 180° and less than 360°. (b) No, since the maximum sum of two obtuse angles is 179° + 179° = 358° < 360°.
provenance_excerpt:
Can we have two obtuse angles whose sum is
(a) a reflex angle? Why or why not?
(b) a complete angle? Why or why not?

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Write the name of
(a) vertices
(b) edges
(c) faces of the prism shown in Fig. 2.48.
answer_key:
(a) Vertices: A, B, C, D, E, F
(b) Edges: AB, BC, CA, DE, EF, FD, AD, BE, CF
(c) Faces: △ABC, △DEF, rectangles ABED, BCFE, CADF
answer_explanation: A triangular prism has 6 vertices, 9 edges, and 5 faces (2 triangular + 3 rectangular).

subject: Mathematics
question_text: How many edges, faces and vertices are there in a sphere?
answer_explanation: A sphere has no edges, no vertices, and one continuous curved face.
provenance_excerpt: How many edges, faces and vertices are there in a sphere?

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Draw all the diagonals of a pentagon ABCDE and name them.
answer_key: AC, AD, BD, BE, CE (5 diagonals)
answer_explanation: A pentagon has 5(5-3)/2 = 5 diagonals. They are AC, AD, BD, BE, CE.
